source: Smelling Salts Review — extracted from conversation history line 94 (Tim's explicit correction)
name: Learning Loop — Universal Protocol Correction
description: Tim explicitly corrected a fundamental misunderstanding: The Learning Loop is NOT a Ruby Red-specific protocol. It is a universal recursive improvement engine that can be pointed at ANY North Star. Ruby Red is ONE example of a North Star — the one used when the loop is pointed at Maven/Grace. Different North Stars for different projects: CashCo, Digger Cafe, Bingo City, etc.

correction_details.incorrect_assumption: The Learning Loop is a Maven/Grace-specific protocol designed for Ruby Red
correction_details.correct_understanding: The Learning Loop is a universal recursive improvement engine. It requires a North Star declaration before running. The North Star determines what the loop optimizes for.
correction_details.tims_words: The Learning Loop is not Ruby Red specific. It's a universal protocol. You declare a North Star, then run the loop. Ruby Red is the North Star when you point it at Maven/Grace.

north_star_examples.north_star: Ruby Red — the 35-45 year old CFO of the household
north_star_examples.optimization_target: 100% lift in Ruby's net financial position in 90 days

north_star_examples.north_star: TBD — likely the CashCo borrower persona
TBD — likely the cafe customer/community member
TBD — likely the Bingo City resident/user

recursive_phase_self_improvement.description: Each step in the Learning Loop has its own recursive sub-loop. Each step starts with baseline grading and must reach 'primal' before moving on.

grading_system.primal: The step has reached its minimum viable quality — it can support the next step
grading_system.primal_plus: The step exceeds minimum viable quality — it actively enhances the next step

recursive_phase_self_improvement.fractal_nature: The loop within the loop within the loop. The same improvement pattern repeats at every scale — feature level, session level, system level.

implications_for_vault: Learning Loop schemas should NOT reference Ruby Red as a hardcoded North Star
Learning Loop documentation should include North Star declaration as Step 0
Race reports should include which North Star was declared for that race
The V20 Truth Enforcement Schema should enforce North Star declaration, not Ruby Red specifically
